title: Fix expired-session redirect
spec_text:
## Goal
Expired sessions redirect users to login while preserving the original destination.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Expired sessions return HTTP 302 to /login.
- The redirect includes a next query parameter with the original path.
- Authenticated sessions continue to access the protected route.

## Out of Scope
- Changing password reset behavior.
- Replacing the authentication middleware architecture.
- Disabling authentication checks.

## Risk Areas
- authentication
- session handling

## Expected Scope
small_bugfix

title: Pin billing webhook dependency safely
spec_text:
## Goal
Pin the billing webhook client dependency to a safe compatible version without weakening signature verification.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Dependency version is pinned to a non-vulnerable compatible range.
- Webhook signature verification remains required.
- Existing webhook tests continue to cover invalid signatures.
- Configuration changes are documented for operators.

## Out of Scope
- Replacing the payment provider.
- Disabling webhook signature checks.
- Changing invoice creation semantics.

## Risk Areas
- billing
- dependency management
- webhook security

## Expected Scope
medium_change

title: Extract HTTP response parsing into a utility function
spec_text:
## Goal
Extract the JSON response parsing logic from route handlers into a reusable utility function to reduce duplication across controllers.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A parse_response utility is created in app/utils/response.py.
- All route handlers that parse JSON responses use the new utility.
- Existing route behavior is unchanged (same status codes and response shapes).
- The utility handles non-200 responses gracefully.

## Out of Scope
- Changing response format or status codes.
- Adding new API endpoints.
- Modifying authentication logic.

## Risk Areas
- code quality
- refactoring
- API contracts

## Expected Scope
small_refactor

title: Add idempotency key support to invoice creation
spec_text:
## Goal
Add optional idempotency key parameter to invoice creation endpoint so that duplicate API calls do not create multiple invoices.

## Acceptance Criteria
- POST /invoices accepts an optional X-Idempotency-Key header.
- Duplicate calls with the same idempotency key return the original invoice.
- Invoices without idempotency key are created normally (backwards compatible).
- Idempotency key is stored with the invoice record.

## Out of Scope
- Changing invoice number format or existing invoice fields.
- Adding idempotency to non-invoice endpoints.
- Modifying payment processing logic.

## Risk Areas
- API contracts
- data integrity
- backwards compatibility

## Expected Scope
medium_change

title: Add unit tests for dashboard chart rendering
spec_text:
## Goal
Add unit tests for the chart rendering component to ensure data is transformed correctly before being passed to the charting library.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Unit tests cover chart data transformation logic.
- {'Tests verify edge cases': 'empty data, null values, large datasets.'}
- All existing tests continue to pass.
- Test files are placed in the appropriate test directory.

## Out of Scope
- Changing chart rendering behavior.
- Modifying the charting library or its configuration.
- Adding integration or end-to-end tests.

## Risk Areas
- code quality
- testing

## Expected Scope
small_change

title: Add client-side input validation for the login form
spec_text:
## Goal
Add client-side validation to the login form so that empty or malformed inputs are rejected before being sent to the server.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Email field validates format on blur.
- Password field requires at least 8 characters.
- Submit button is disabled until all fields are valid.
- Server-side validation remains in place as a fallback.

## Out of Scope
- Changing server-side validation logic.
- Adding new API endpoints.
- Modifying the authentication flow.

## Risk Areas
- UX
- input validation

## Expected Scope
small_change

title: Upgrade all dependencies to latest stable versions
spec_text:
## Goal
Upgrade all project dependencies to their latest stable versions while maintaining compatibility and passing all tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All dependencies are pinned to a specific stable version.
- Django and all third-party packages are tested for compatibility.
- Existing tests pass without modification.
- Changelog or release notes are reviewed for breaking changes.

## Out of Scope
- Changing application code to adapt to new dependency versions.
- Upgrading Django LTS version (stay on current major version).
- Removing or replacing major dependencies.

## Risk Areas
- dependency management
- backwards compatibility

## Expected Scope
medium_change

title: Migrate all API calls to REST conventions
spec_text:
## Goal
Migrate all frontend API calls to use consistent conventions: named exports, typed responses, and structured error objects.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All API calls use named async functions instead of default exports.
- Response types are consistent across all endpoints.
- Error responses return consistent shape with status and message.
- TypeScript types are exported for all API responses.

## Out of Scope
- Changing API endpoint URLs or protocols.
- Modifying backend logic or database schema.
- Adding new features beyond API call migration.

## Risk Areas
- code quality
- API contracts
- refactoring

## Expected Scope
large_refactor

title: Fix slow query performance in the invoice listing endpoint
spec_text:
## Goal
Fix the N+1 query problem in the invoice listing endpoint so that related customer data is fetched in a single query instead of one per row.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Invoice list uses select_related or prefetch_related to reduce queries.
- Response time for 100 invoices is under 200ms.
- Existing tests for invoice listing continue to pass.
- No changes to API response format or field names.

## Out of Scope
- Adding new filters or sorting options to the listing.
- Modifying the invoice creation endpoint.
- Adding caching layers.

## Risk Areas
- performance
- database

## Expected Scope
small_bugfix

title: Add rate limiting to the public API endpoints
spec_text:
## Goal
Add rate limiting middleware to all public API endpoints so that excessive requests from a single client are throttled to prevent abuse.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Rate limiting middleware is applied to all public routes.
- Rate limit is configurable (requests per minute per client IP).
- Requests exceeding the limit return HTTP 429 with a Retry-After header.
- Existing tests continue to pass.

## Out of Scope
- Rate limiting on authenticated internal endpoints.
- Changing business logic or data handling.
- Adding user authentication or authorization.

## Risk Areas
- security
- rate limiting
- middleware

## Expected Scope
medium_change
